title: Scalpel Configuration
description: Configuration schema for Scalpel v2 — extensible codebase health scanning, team orchestration, scoring, monitoring, and memory.

$schema.description: Reference to this JSON Schema for editor autocompletion and validation.

version.description: Configuration format version. Must match a supported Scalpel release.

scan.description: Controls which dimensions are scanned, paths to ignore, and custom secret patterns.

dimensions.description: Select, exclude, or extend the scan dimensions.

include.description: Dimensions to include. Use ["all"] to include every built-in dimension, or list specific ones.

exclude.description: Dimensions to skip even if they appear in include. Useful with include: ["all"].

custom.description: User-defined scan dimensions that extend the built-in set.

ignore_paths.description: Glob patterns for directories and files the scanner should skip entirely.

secret_patterns.description: Additional regex patterns to detect exposed secrets, beyond the built-in set (AWS keys, Stripe keys, etc.).

team.description: Controls agent team assembly — size, models, and custom specialist roles.

max_agents.description: Maximum number of parallel agents the Architect may spawn in Phase 4.

default_model.description: Model used for implementation agents (Phase 4 workers, monitoring).

architect_model.description: Model used for strategic reasoning (Phase 2 questions, Phase 3 team design).

custom_roles.description: Additional specialist roles the Architect can assign when conditions match.

scoring.description: Extend or adjust the health score algorithm with custom rules and thresholds.

custom_rules.description: User-defined scoring rules. Each rule runs a bash command; exit 0 means pass.

thresholds.description: Score boundaries for health ratings. Each value is the minimum score for that tier.

at_risk.description: Minimum score for 'At Risk' rating. Scores below this are 'Critical'.

monitoring.description: Settings for the /loop continuous monitoring mode (Phase 4+).

interval_minutes.description: How often the monitor re-scans the project during a /loop session.

alert_threshold.description: Health score drop (in points) that triggers an alert during monitoring.

auto_start.description: If true, Scalpel enters monitoring mode automatically after Phase 5 completes.

memory.description: Controls persistent session memory across Scalpel runs.

enabled.description: Whether Scalpel writes session summaries to the memory file after each run.

path.description: Path to the JSONL memory file, relative to the project root.

customDimension.description: A user-defined scan dimension with a bash command and scoring behavior.

name.description: Unique identifier for this dimension (snake_case recommended).

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this dimension checks.

command.description: Bash command to evaluate this dimension. Exit 0 = pass, non-zero = fail. Stdout is captured for the report.

scoring.description: Points awarded on pass or fail.

pass.description: Points added to the health score when the command exits 0.

fail.description: Points added to the health score when the command exits non-zero (typically 0).

customRole.description: A custom specialist role that the Architect can assign to an agent when conditions are met.

trigger.description: Condition that must be true for this role to be included. Evaluated as a bash test (exit 0 = include).

jurisdiction.description: Glob patterns for files this role is allowed to read and modify.

prompt.description: System prompt injected for this specialist agent. Defines its expertise and operating rules.

customScoringRule.description: A custom scoring rule that adds or deducts points based on a bash command result.

check.description: Bash command to evaluate. Exit 0 = rule passes (bonus applied). Non-zero = rule fails (deduction applied).

deduction.description: Points deducted when the check fails (must be negative or zero).

bonus.description: Points awarded when the check passes.

message.description: Human-readable message shown in the report when this rule triggers.
